mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month increase in INDPRO signals expanding industrial capacity utilization that translates into higher corporate earnings, driving positive 20-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO month-over-month changes show a Rank IC below 0.03 with 20-day forward returns over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the effective federal funds rate drops below 0.25%, it signals binding zero lower bound constraints that force investors out of cash and into equities, generating positive 60-day S&P 500 returns until the threshold is crossed.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS < 0.25%, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are not significantly positive (one-sided t-test p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts create a reinforcing demand loop where consumer spending and residential investment jointly lift corporate revenue expectations, producing positive 20-day S&P 500 returns that neither series achieves in isolation.
failure_condition: The bivariate interaction of RSAFS and HOUST month-over-month changes fails to achieve a t-statistic > 2.0 in predicting 20-day returns over any 2-year rolling window, or the marginal R-squared of the interaction term is < 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE exceeds 6%, a month-over-month increase in PAYEMS signals rapid labor market repair that reduces equity risk premiums; when UNRATE is below 6%, the same PAYEMS increase contains no marginal information, yielding flat returns.
failure_condition: In periods with UNRATE > 6%, PAYEMS month-over-month increases do not produce positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of instances, or the mean return is not significantly above zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly decline in real GDP (GDPC1) signals contracting aggregate demand that compresses top-line revenue growth across the corporate sector, generating negative 60-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Quarter-over-quarter GDPC1 declines fail to predict negative 60-day S&P 500 returns with a Rank IC more negative than -0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO falls by more than 1.5% over a rolling 3-month window, it signals acute industrial contraction that raises recession probabilities and drives negative 20-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: When INDPRO declines > 1.5% over 3 months, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rising CPIAUCSL month-over-month and a falling FEDFUNDS rate generates negative real rate shocks that squeeze corporate margins and reduce forward earnings multiples, producing negative 5-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term between CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS month-over-month changes fails to produce a Rank IC more negative than -0.03 over 5-day returns in any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month drop in housing starts below 500 thousand units signals collapsing residential investment that drags construction and material sectors lower, generating negative 5-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: When HOUST falls below 500 thousand units, S&P 500 5-day forward returns are not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.05) or the hit rate of negative returns is below 55%.
